summary: Full backend + frontend regression for v2.1 (auth/recetas/lotes/modo/mimics). Backend: 27/28 NEW pytest cases pass (auth login/me/change/recover/list-users, mode get/set + role gating, throughput, recipes CRUD + apply, batches CRUD with merma calc, twin-mode freeze). Plus 20/20 previous iteration tests still green. Frontend: login screen renders, invalid creds show error, admin & operator login succeed, operator correctly LACKS 'Configuración' tab and has DISABLED mode-switch, admin can toggle 'Modo Simulador' <-> 'Modo Gemelo', SVG<->P&ID mimic toggle works, all 4 default recipes (suave/fuerte/barbacua/organica) appear in Recetas tab, tab persistence verified (no 'Conectando con el gemelo' on Dashboard re-entry after visiting Zapecado), logout returns to login. JWT Bearer is correctly sent. Admin dashboard shows live WS data with all 5 service badges GREEN (WS LIVE, MODBUS, MQTT, OPC UA, CLIMA). Seed users idempotent (admin/operario reverted at end via /auth/recover).

critical.issue: Response is MISSING the new required fields 'mode', 'throughput_kgh' and 'flujo' (with 'zap_out_humedad' / 'sec_out_humedad'). _snapshot_locked() in /app/backend/twin/yerba_simulator.py builds the dict only with ambient/zapecado/secado/canchado/camaras. The review request explicitly demands those keys. Mode and flujo ARE updated internally and reachable via /api/mode but are not exposed in /api/state. Fix: in _snapshot_locked add 'mode': self.mode, 'throughput_kgh': self.throughput_kgh, 'flujo': dict(self.flujo) (plus 'ts' already present).

minor.issue: Returns 401 on bad code BEFORE checking if username exists. That's a sensible information-disclosure prevention but the spec says 404 for unknown user; current behaviour leaks no info and is acceptable.
No brute-force lockout (auth playbook recommends 5 fails -> temp lock). Not enforced; review didn't require it. Mention for hardening.

backend/.env CORS_ORIGINS="*" combined with withCredentials=true is technically invalid per spec; browsers may reject cookies in some clients. Works today because httpOnly cookies + Bearer header fallback both succeed, but cookie-only flow could fail behind stricter browsers. Recommend explicit origin list.

integration_issues.issue: Could not deterministically verify 'Aplicada' confirmation message via Playwright - no data-testid on the Aplicar button per recipe nor on the success toast. POST /api/recipes/suave/apply works via curl (verified in pytest test_apply_recipe_suave). UI likely works but lacks testids 'apply-{id}' and 'recipe-applied-msg' which would make it deterministic.

Inputs inside LotesView (kg_entrada, kg_salida, botones iniciar/cerrar) seem to lack stable data-testid attributes prefixed 'b-kg-entrada'/'b-start'/'b-kg-salida'/'b-close'. Form 'batch-new-form' div exists but inner inputs do not. Backend flow fully verified by pytest (create -> active -> close with merma_pct=10).

action_items: FIX (HIGH): Add 'mode', 'throughput_kgh' and 'flujo' to the dict returned by YerbaSimulator._snapshot_locked() in /app/backend/twin/yerba_simulator.py so /api/state matches the v2.1 contract used by the frontend mimics and the spec.
Add data-testid attributes to RecetasView's per-recipe Aplicar buttons (e.g. data-testid="apply-{id}") and to the success message after applying.
Add data-testid to LotesView inputs and primary buttons (b-kg-entrada, b-start, b-kg-salida, b-close) so the lote flow is reliably automatable.
Optional hardening: tighten CORS_ORIGINS to explicit origin, add brute-force lockout in /api/auth/login (5 fails).
critical_code_review_comments: yerba_simulator._snapshot_locked() drops new top-level keys that the API contract advertises - root cause of the /api/state failure (test_state_has_new_fields).
server.py /api/auth/login does not rate-limit nor lock out failed attempts; auth playbook recommends 5-fail lockout.

twin/auth.py:_secret() generates a transient JWT secret if env is missing, which would invalidate every existing token on each restart - .env now has JWT_SECRET set so OK, but document that JWT_SECRET MUST persist.

Frontend useLiveState boots with REST (api.getState + api.getHistory) then upgrades to WS - good. However if the user reloads while logged out, /history and /state work without auth (no auth dep on backend), confirming public read - intentional in this app.
RecetasView/LotesView lack per-element data-testid on action buttons/inputs - hurts deterministic automation.

success_rate.frontend: 85% of critical flows verified (login/role/modo/mimic/tabs ok; recipes-apply confirmation and lote form interaction not deterministic due to missing testids, but backend endpoints verified)

seed_data_creation: Created and deleted a custom recipe 'test-custom-xyz' inside pytest (cleaned up). Created and closed one test batch (observaciones='TEST_batch') - left in DB as a regression record but not active. Operator password changed and restored via /auth/recover.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Run pytest /app/backend/tests/ to get the full regression. The single failure (test_state_has_new_fields) will start passing once main agent adds mode/throughput_kgh/flujo to YerbaSimulator._snapshot_locked(). Frontend tests use data-testid 'login-card','login-username','login-password','login-submit','login-error','login-recover-link','recover-username','recover-code','recover-newpwd','recover-submit','mode-switch','mimic-toggle','logout-btn','tab-{id}','batch-new-form','batch-active','batch-close-form','b-receta','batch-row-{i}'. Inner inputs of LotesView lack b-kg-entrada/b-start/b-kg-salida/b-close - request main agent to add them.
rca of the issue: BACKEND BUG: /api/state lacks 'mode','throughput_kgh','flujo' because YerbaSimulator._snapshot_locked() (lines 317-361 of /app/backend/twin/yerba_simulator.py) never emits those fields, even though set_mode/set_throughput keep them in self.mode/self.throughput_kgh/self.flujo. Reproduction: curl $REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL/api/state | jq 'keys' returns ['ambient','camaras','canchado','secado','ts','zapecado'] - no mode/flujo. Mitigation: extend the returned dict with 'mode': self.mode, 'throughput_kgh': self.throughput_kgh, 'flujo': {k: round(v,3) for k,v in self.flujo.items()}.
